message:
fix(lab,cancel): six correctness fixes across testing infrastructure

1. symbol_cancel: CleanupCoordinator::cleanup() now removes the handler
   from the handlers map BEFORE executing the callback. Previously the
   handler was invoked while the handlers RwLock was held (read), so any
   callback that tried to register/remove handlers would deadlock. Test
   verifies the write lock is available during callback execution.

2. chaos: ChaosRng::next_delay_in_range() overflowed on large Duration
   ranges (>2^64 nanoseconds) because it cast u128 nanos to u64 before
   computing the random offset. Now works entirely in u128 space using
   two u64 random values combined into u128. Adds helper
   nanos_to_duration_saturating() and a test with 40-billion-second
   ranges.

3. injection: LabInjectionRunner now marks runs where the injection
   target point was never reached as AssertionFailed instead of Success.
   A future that completes normally means cancellation was never
   injected, which is not a valid test of that injection point.

4. instrumented_future: InjectionRunner::run_with_injection() resets
   current_mode to Recording after completing a run. Previously the mode
   was left in whatever state the last phase set it to, causing stale
   state to leak into subsequent runs on the same runner instance.

5. replay: find_divergence() no longer treats sequence-number-only
   differences as divergence. Sequence numbers are non-deterministic
   metadata assigned by the tracing infrastructure and should not affect
   structural comparison of traces. Also adds tests for both
   find_divergence and traces_equivalent confirming seq is ignored.

6. scenario_runner: ScenarioRunResult::passed() now requires the
   quiescent flag to be true. A scenario that passes all oracles but
   leaves the runtime in a non-quiescent state (pending tasks, unfired
   timers) is not a valid pass. Failure messages now include the
   quiescence violation.

7. virtual_time_wheel: cancel() now validates the handle still
   corresponds to a pending timer in the heap. A stale handle (from an
   already-expired timer) previously inserted a phantom entry into the
   cancelled set, causing len() and is_empty() to undercount or report
   negative pending timers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
